Article Title: Crackdown on paper leak: Govt planning to tweak anti-cheating law by increasing jail term
Article Text:
In the expected amendment, the government is likely to increase the jail.

Poll Vote & Share your view What measure should be prioritized to prevent exam paper leaks? Stricter laws Better technology More awareness campaigns 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: The central government will introduce an amendment to the law meant to prevent paper leaks and cheating in public examinations to provide for fast-track courts for paper leak cases.In the expected amendment, the government is also likely to increase the jail term for offenders from the current 10-year maximum, sources told TOI. The proposed bill will be taken up by the Cabinet this afternoon.The jail terms in the existing Act will be increased across three sections and fast track courts will be made mandatory for trial in the Bill in all such cases.The Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024, which was enacted to curb paper leaks, cheating and organised malpractices in public examinations such as UPSC, SSC and NTA tests, provides for penalties, including up to 10 years' imprisonment and a Rs 1 crore fine.The proposed Bill is in line with PM Modi's late Thursday announcement that the Union Cabinet will on Friday discuss a Bill to further strengthen the anti-cheating law and ensure stricter punishment for offenders.Hours after ordering the establishment of fast-track courts, in a recorded message posted on social media at midnight, PM said that based on the discussion a bill will be moved on Monday, when the second week of the current Parliament session begins, and government will seek its passage at the earliest.“Fast-track courts and strict punishment will be discussed in the Cabinet tomorrow and it will be given final shape after the discussion,” Modi said, while underlining that government has been working on addressing the concerns related to medical entrance exams paper leak over the last two and a half months. He said that government had so far focused on ensuring that students did not lose a year and decided to conduct exams for 22 lakh students, using “all its might”, with results also declared.Modi’s message through the unusual mode came minutes after government replaced the education secretary amid the ongoing protests against the NEET paper leak.Over the last two days, officials said, PMO has been engaged with the education ministry over the NEET exam, retest and K Radhakrishnan committee on NTA reforms, with over two dozen queries flowing from Seva Teerth.
Article Summary: In the expected amendment, the government is likely to increase the jail term for offenders from the current 10-year maximum. The jail terms in the existing Act will be increased across three sections. Fast track courts will be made mandatory for trial in the Bill in all such cases.

Article Title: ‘No plans to enter airline business:’ Adani Group
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Adani Group has denied any plans to enter the airline business. An Adani Enterprises Limited spokesperson said on Friday: “We would like to categorically deny the recent media reports and market speculation suggesting that Adani Enterprises is planning to launch an airline. These reports are entirely baseless and factually incorrect. Adani Enterprises is not evaluating any proposal to enter the airline business.”The statement comes amid govt being reportedly being uncomfortable with a country of the size and economic potential of India having a duopoly of airlines — IndiGo and Tata’s Air India group. Akasa is also coming up in a big way. However, the West Asia crisis coupled with other India-specific issues like closure of Pakistan airspace and the rupee’s relentless crash have made things tougher for airlines here than they are for carriers globally. Both IndiGo and AI Group have slashed flights due to escalating operational costs. Smaller players like SpiceJet are struggling to survive.In this context, India is considering ways to make it easier to launch new airlines. One of these steps reportedly were allowing the operators of India’s busiest airports — Delhi and Mumbai — to hold more than 10% stake in airlines. The GMR and Adani groups run these two hubs, respectively.Both these conglomerates have major presence in aviation that goes beyond just running airports as they have maintenance, repair overhaul (MRO) and other businesses.The Adani Group has tied up with Embraer to make-in-India regional jets. However, the Brazilian aerospace company has linked setting up and operationalising the proposed final assembly line (FAL) to getting a firm order of at least 200 jets. Dholera in Gujarat has been selected as the site for this proposed FAL.Billionaire Gautam Adani-led conglomerate is the biggest private airport operator in India (in terms of number of airports). It operates hubs in Mumbai, Navi Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Lucknow, Jaipur, Guwahati, Mangalore and Thiruvananthapuram.
Article Summary: The statement comes amid govt being reportedly being uncomfortable with a country of the size and economic potential of India having a duopoly of airlines. The West Asia crisis coupled with other India-specific issues like closure of Pakistan airspace and the rupee’s relentless crash have made things tougher for airlines.

Article Title: 'Paper leaks are no ordinary matter': What PM Modi said in his midnight video message
Article Text:
PM Modi promises tougher action in midnight address

NEW DELHI: With CJP-led protests over NEET-UG 2026 examination irregularities showing no signs of easing, Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday said the government would take stricter action against paper leaks."Friends, I know that paper leaks are not an ordinary matter. For lakhs of students and their parents, it is extremely painful," PM Modi said in a self-recorded video posted on X.PM Modi said the Union Cabinet would consider a draft Bill proposing Fast-Track Courts and stringent punishment for offenders."In the last two and a half months since the paper leak incidents, several major steps have been taken. The culprits have been caught; they are currently in jail," he added.PM Modi highlighted that the government made every effort to ensure that the students did not lose an academic year."Our most important responsibility was to ensure that a whole year for the students is not wasted. It was very important to conduct the exams immediately. The government used its full strength to arrange exams for nearly 22 lakh students in the shortest possible time. And just five or six days ago, on the 19th, the results were also announced.News of the happiness of successful students is coming from all over the country. "PM Modi said that the government has decided to take stronger measures to tackle paper leaks."However, we are not among those who are satisfied with just that. And therefore, today I have issued directions to the departments to set up Fast-Track Courts. The departments worked tirelessly and, late last night, provided me with a draft. This draft, which includes provisions for Fast-Track Courts and stringent punishments, will be discussed in the Cabinet tomorrow," he added."After incorporating suggestions from Cabinet colleagues, it will be given a final shape. Efforts will be made to get this Bill passed in the House as soon as possible, as the second week of the Parliament session begins on Monday," he said.The PM also urged activist Sonam Wangchuk to follow doctors' advice, recover well and gain weight as soon as possible."I urge Sonam ji to follow his routine as per the doctors' advice and regain his old weight as soon as possible. I pray to the Lord that Sonam ji remains healthy," he said in a post on X.Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike on Friday at Medanta Hospital in the presence of Union ministers J P Nadda and Jitendra Singh, along with senior leaders of the Apex Body of Leh Ladakh.In a post on X, Wangchuk said he decided to call off the fast after prolonged negotiations on various conditions and in view of the possibility of violence in the country.The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), which has been spearheading the protest at Jantar Mantar, welcomed Wangchuk's decision but said its agitation would continue until Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.
Article Summary: PM Narendra Modi said the government would take stricter action against paper leaks. Union Cabinet would consider a draft Bill proposing Fast-Track Courts and stringent punishment for offenders. PM Modi urged activist Sonam Wangchuk to follow doctors' advice, recover well and gain weight as soon as possible.

Article Title: CJP, Centre set for 12:30 pm talks at Constitution Club; Pradhan's resignation among key demand
Article Text:
CJP-Centre meeting to be held today (ANI)

'Peaceful protest will continue'

NEW DELHI: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) delegation will meet Union ministers at the Constitution Club of India at 12.30 pm on Friday, after the Centre agreed to hold talks at a neutral venue—a key demand raised by the outfit during its ongoing agitation over the NEET row.The meeting comes a day after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike at Medanta Hospital in the presence of Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh, paving the way for formal discussions between the government and the protesters.Confirming the development, CJP chief spokesperson Saurav Das said the organisation welcomed the government's decision to shift the talks to a neutral venue."Our protest will continue; Constitution Club of India chosen as venue for meeting with government ministers, hoping for an open-minded dialogue," Das told reporters on Thursday.CJP spokesperson Vaishnavi Gaur said the delegation would place the same demands it has been raising since the protests began."We will be putting forward our demands, which we have been consistent with from the first day," she said.According to Gaur, the key demands include the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, compensation for the families affected by the NEET controversy, and withdrawal of all FIRs registered against what she described as "innocent protesters."Following Wangchuk's decision to end his fast, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke thanked him for what he called an extraordinary act of courage."We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days. Thank you for your extraordinary courage and sacrifice. By putting your own life on the line, you awakened the conscience of an entire nation. Your life is far too precious to this country," Dipke wrote in a post on X.Despite Wangchuk ending his fast, the CJP has maintained that its peaceful protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar will continue until its demands, including Pradhan's resignation, are addressed.
Article Summary: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) delegation will meet Union ministers at the Constitution Club of India at 12.30 pm on Friday. Meeting comes a day after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike at Medanta Hospital.

Article Title: Govt reshuffles top bureaucrats in ministries: Education secretary shifted out
Article Text:
Dr. Vineet Joshi replaced as education secretary

NEW DELHI: The Centre on Thursday carried out a major bureaucratic reshuffle, effecting 13 secretary-level appointments across ministries and departments, including key changes in the education ministry, amid continuing protests over the alleged NEET paper leak. Senior IAS officer Naresh Pal Gangwar has been appointed as secretary of the department of higher education and T K Anil Kumar as secretary of the department of school education and literacy, amid continuing protests over the alleged NEET paper leak.According to an order issued by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (ACC), Gangwar, a 1994-batch IAS officer of the Rajasthan cadre, will replace Vineet Joshi as secretary of the department of higher education. Joshi, a 1992-batch IAS officer of the Manipur cadre, has been appointed secretary in the ministry of panchayati raj.Gangwar was serving as secretary in the department of animal husbandry and dairying under the ministry of fisheries, animal husbandry and dairying. An engineer by training, he holds a BTech in Electronics and Communication from the University of Roorkee, an MTech in Communication from IIT Delhi, and an MA in Economics from the University of Rajasthan.He has previously served in several senior positions in the Rajasthan government and the Centre, including principal secretary in Rajasthan and joint secretary in the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.The ACC also appointed T K Anil Kumar, a 1996-batch IAS officer of the Karnataka cadre and additional secretary in the Ministry of Rural Development, as secretary of the Department of School Education and Literacy.The government order did not cite any reason for the appointments. The reshuffle comes amid continuing protests by the CJP and students over the alleged irregularities in the NEET examination, with demonstrators demanding accountability and reforms in the examination system.Notably, the reshuffle went well beyond the education ministry. Katikithala Srinivas moves from housing and urban affairs to head the ministry of development of north eastern region, while Piyush Goyal shifts from secretary, ministry of mines, to secretary general of the national human rights commission; with Keshav Chandra taking over at Mines.In animal husbandry and dairying, vacated by Gangwar's move, Sushil Kumar Lohani has been posted as Officer on Special Duty before formally taking over as Secretary. Other new appointments include D. Thara at the Department of Capital Development (Housing and Urban Affairs), Chandra Bhushan Kumar at Labour and Employment, Satendra Singh at Urban Development, and Suchindra Misra at the National Commission for Minorities.The ACC also approved in-situ upgradation to Special Secretary rank, with Secretary-level pay, for four officers in their current postings: Mritunjay Kumar Narayan (Registrar General of India), Santosh Kumar Yadav (Chairman, NHAI), Rajeev Singh Thakur (Adviser, NITI Aayog), and Dilip Kumar (DG Training, Skill Development Ministry).
Article Summary: Senior IAS officer Naresh Pal Gangwar appointed as secretary of the department of higher education. Vineet Joshi replaced as education secretary in the ministry of panchayati raj. Shuffle comes amid continuing protests over alleged irregularities in the NEET examination.

Article Title: MPs ask why govt didn’t inform owners of non-E20 vehicles about mileage loss
Article Text:
MPs questioned why the government did not warn owners of non-E20 vehicles about possible mileage loss.

NEW DELHI: Members of the parliamentary committee on transport Thursday asked top government officials on why no effort was made to inform owners of non-E20 compliant vehicles about potential mileage loss due to use of 20% ethanol blended petrol in these vehicles.TOI has learnt that some members also asked officials whether any economic analysis has been carried out to assess whether E20 fuel has had any gain to individual vehicle owners even as they agreed that the increased ethanol blending in petrol has helped the country to reduce dependence on imported crude.The panel headed by JDU’s Sanjay Jha had called officials from road transport and petroleum ministries, representatives from vehicle testing agencies and oil marketing companies, amid the ongoing controversy over impact of E20 on mileage and engines of old vehicles.Some members asked why there is so much sudden opposition and criticism when govt claims that over 19% ethanol blended fuel has been sold for more than two years.“Some members pointed out that since the controversy has peaked, all departments, vehicle manufacturers, oil companies and test agencies have aligned. They also sought exact mileage loss due to E20 fuel. Road transport ministry officials said mileage loss is between 2 and 6% as opposed to some hearsay claiming to be 25%,” said a person privy to the discussion.One of the participants said that officials pointed out two issues that may have triggered the fresh row were — the BIS notification for higher blended ethanol E22, E25 and E30, and sale of adulterated fuels.
Article Summary: MPs questioned why the government did not warn owners of non-E20 vehicles about possible mileage loss. Some members also asked officials whether any economic analysis has been carried out to assess whether E20 fuel has had any gain to individual vehicle owners.

Article Title: NIA arrests absconding PFI district chief in Kerala's Sreenivasan murder case
Article Text:
NIA arrested a key absconder in Kerala’s Sreenivasan murder case

NEW DELHI: National Investigation Agency (NIA) ion Thursday arrested a key absconder in Kerala’s Sreenivasan murder case, linked with the banned Popular Front of India (PFI) terrorist organisation.Muhammadali K.P, the PFI Palakkad district president, was taken into custody on Thursday morning at the IGI Airport, New Delhi, on his arrival from Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, where he had been hiding for the past four years. With this, the total number of accused arrested in the case now stands at 67.Among the 65 accused chargesheeted so far in the case, Muhammadali K.P is facing charges under relevant sections of IPC and UA (P) Act.Hailing from Palakkad district in Kerala, the wanted accused was a prominent perpetrator of the murder conspiracy and had also harboured the assailants involved in the brutal killing of Sreenivasan on April 16, 2022. NIA had declared a reward of Rs 3 lakh for information leading to his arrest and an Interpol Red Notice was also issued against him.Sreenivasan was killed by PFI cadres in pursuance of the PFI conspiracy to unleash terror with the nefarious agenda of establishing Islamic Rule in India by 2047. NIA, which had registered the case in September 2022, found during investigation that the accused involved in the case had conspired to create a communal divide through violent extremism and Jihad in the country.Investigations had further revealed that PFI was operating through various Wings and units established for the said purpose, and was using its offices, campuses, facilities and infrastructure to impart arms training to selected cadres in the guise of physical education, yoga training etc through its physical education Wing. The reporters wing was tasked with collecting information about prospective targets, while the actual terror acts were executed by the ‘service teams’ who trained in handling of weapons.
Article Summary: National Investigation Agency (NIA) ion Thursday arrested a key absconder in Kerala’s Sreenivasan murder case. Muhammadali K.P, the PFI Palakkad district president, was taken into custody on Thursday morning at the IGI Airport, New Delhi. With this, the total number of accused arrested in the case now stands at 67.

Article Title: 2 new secretaries in education ministry, Vineet Joshi moved to panchayati raj
Article Text:
Joshi moved to panchayati raj

NEW DELHI: Amid ongoing protests over NEET-UG paper leak issue, govt Thursday named new secretaries for higher and school education departments, with incumbent Vineet Joshi, who had charge of both, moved to low-profile panchayati raj department.While animal husbandry secretary Naresh Pal Gangwar was appointed the new secretary for higher education, TK Anil Kumar will be the new school education secretary. The changes mark an overhaul of sorts in education ministry bureaucracy, as Abhishek Singh was earlier named head of the National Testing Agency , which conducts NEET, CUET and other exams.In early June, CBSE chief Rahul Singh and secretary Himanshu Gupta were transferred out of the National Testing Agency after a controversy over on-screen marking.The secretary-level appointments included 13 new department heads, including a bifurcation of ministry of housing and urban affairs. MoHUA secretary K Srinivas has been shifted to development of north eastern region (DoNER) ministry. D Thara, from Gujarat cadre, has been appointed secretary of the just-created department of capital development, while her batchmate Satendra Singh from Jharkhand will lead the department of urban development.The changes in education ministry are significant as govt has said demand for removal of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan is not under consideration, but steps will be taken to address concerns. The appointments committee of cabinet also desginated Chandra Bhushan Kumar as new labour secretary, while Keshav Chandra will be the new mines secretary. Odisha cadre IAS officer SK Lohani has been designated as animal husbandry secretary.
Article Summary: Vineet Joshi, who had charge of both, moved to low-profile panchayati raj department. The changes mark an overhaul of sorts in education ministry bureaucracy. Abhishek Singh was earlier named head of the National Testing Agency.

Article Title: Road crashes killed a record 1.8 lakh in 2025; 502 people daily
Article Text:
Road crashes claimed a little over 1.8 lakh lives in 2025

Death tol lrises

Road crashes claimed a little over 1.8 lakh lives in 2025, translating into 502 deaths per day, which is the maximum since govt started collating data. While the highest increase in such fatalities was reported in Uttar Pradesh -- from 24,118 in 2024 to 27,550 last year -- Punjab saw a major dip in road deaths -- from 4,759 in 2024 to 3,062 last year.Data that the road transport ministry provided to Lok Sabha on Thursday show that road crashes shot up by 5.3% to over 5.1 lakh in 2025 and fatalities increased by 3.5%. Ministry’s written reply indicates that 18 states and UTs saw a rise in road deaths in 2025 while 17 others reported a decline. Significantly, Lakshadweep has not reported any road death in the past two years.Ministry said data for West Bengal, which reported fewer number of fatalities in road crashes, have been taken from eDAR records as of Jan 2, 2026, while in the case of all others, data have been taken from eDAR records as of July 22. The e-Detailed Accident Report (eDAR) is a digital central accident database management system designed to analyse causes of crashes and devise safety measures. Officials said the overall number of fatalities may increase when the data for West Bengal is updated.Experts say the rise in road deaths despite efforts of govt to bring them down indicates that the current strategies have not proven that effective, even in states like Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh and Assam where non-profit organisations in the sector have collaborated with local govts.On Wednesday, the ministry had informed Rajya Sabha that 6,358 “black corridors” on the National Highway (NH) network were identified during 2023-25, which recorded 37,164 deaths. The maximum such black corridors were in Tamil Nadu (958), followed by UP (671) and Karnataka (607). Among districts, Viluppuram in Tamil Nadu had a maximum of 70 such corridors.As per ministry, unlike isolated black spots, these corridors are continuous stretches of NH that have recorded a higher number of road crashes that led to fatalities and grievous injuries. The identification is expected to help the authorities prioritise engineering interventions and improve road safety outcomes, officials said.
Article Summary: Road crashes claimed a little over 1.8 lakh lives in 2025, translating into 502 deaths per day. The highest increase in such fatalities was reported in Uttar Pradesh. Punjab saw a major dip in road deaths -- from 4,759 in 2024 to 3,062 last year.

Article Title: IndiGo opposes more say for airport operators in airlines
Article Text:
Rahul Bhatia, promoter and MD of India’s largest carrier IndiGo

NEW DELHI: Rahul Bhatia, promoter and MD of India's largest carrier IndiGo , has opposed the proposed move to allow airport operators in India to own and run airlines too. "There is no global precedent for this. It raises massive conflict-of-interest concerns, and it is unclear how such a move would serve consumers' interests," he said at a post earnings call.Bhatia's Thursday comments come as govt is considering to dilute provisions that disallow operators of Delhi and Mumbai airports to own more than 10% stake in a scheduled airline. While the Adani Group, which runs Mumbai airport, is likely to make the first move by picking up stake in an existing airline or investing in a startup, the GMR Group, which operates Delhi's IGIA, could do so at a subsequent stage.The news has resulted in Interglobe Aviation's shares falling for two consecutive days as investors perceive the airline, which controls two-thirds of the domestic aviation market, to be a loser in case the move goes through.Senior aviation ministry officials said a country with the economic potential of India should have multiple airlines and not just a duopoly with IndiGo and Tata's Air India group.Meanwhile, the Competition Commission of India (CCI) Thursday invited public comments on a commitment proposal by IndiGo in a probe following its last Dec schedule collapse.The airline has proposed temporarily releasing domestic slots at airports. "Post such handover, the airport operator/authority may determine re-allocation of such slots/dates for routes, as deemed fit," the CCI notice says.
Article Summary: Rahul Bhatia, promoter and MD of India's largest carrier IndiGo, has opposed the proposed move to allow airport operators in India to own and run airlines too. "There is no global precedent for this. It raises massive conflict-of-interest concerns, and it is unclear how such a move would serve consumers' interests," he said.

Article Title: SC lifts 2-year-old blanket ban on setting up of industries in Taj zone
Article Text:
SC lifted its two-year-old blanket ban on setting up of industries in Taj Trapezium Zone

NEW DELHI: SC Thursday lifted its two-year-old blanket ban on setting up of industries in Taj Trapezium Zone, a 10,400 sq km area spread over five districts in UP and one in Rajasthan, to open avenues for employment generation. The development comes a few months ahead of UP assembly polls.UP govt, through AG R Venkataramani and ASG Aishwarya Bhati told a bench of CJI Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana that industrial activities, especially when it involves establishment of non-polluting industrial units, cannot be brought to a grinding halt.Seeking modification of SC's Oct 14, 2024 order that barred establishment of any industrial unit in the TTZ area without permission of the court, the AG said, "Keeping in mind aspirations of youth and entrepreneurs, SC should have been a little more flexible when there is a cap on air pollution score of each industry which uses electricity or natural gas as fuel."Senior advocate Aparna Bhat opposed the application, saying the TTZ authority had given arbitrary sanctions for establishment of glass industries which did not meet environmental guidelines. Senior advocate and amicus curiae Liz Mathews suggested to SC that the 410 pending applications for establishment/expansion/relocation of industries in TTZ can be processed by involving green experts from Central Empowered Committee (CEC) and the National Environmental Engineering Research Institute (NEERI).The bench accepted the suggestion after the law officers said the authority is agreeable to any condition for the protection of the environment and the Taj Mahal . It said TTZ authority's Industry Technical Evaluation Committee will involve a domain expert each from CEC and NEERI in the scrutiny of 410 applications."We are giving veto power to the experts. The applications would be entertained by TTZ authority only after the two independent experts from CEC and NEERI concur with the non-pollution character of the industry that is sought to be established," the bench said.It added that in case of dissent from any of the two experts, the proposal would be placed before SC for final adjudication. Even if the experts concurred on setting up of the industry, it would be placed before SC for final clearance, if amicus curiae felt that the matter required judicial scrutiny.The districts falling in the TTZ are Agra, Firozabad, Mathura, Hathras, Etah and Bharatpur.
Article Summary: SC lifted its two-year-old blanket ban on setting up of industries in Taj Trapezium Zone. The 10,400 sq km area is spread over five districts in UP and one in Rajasthan. The development comes a few months ahead of UP assembly polls.

Article Title: Pvt investment in space sector crosses $618 million, 17 startups given nod for space activities: Minister Jitendra Singh to RS
Article Text:
India's space sector has witnessed unprecedented momentum with private investment crossing $618.5 million

NEW DELHI: India's space sector has witnessed unprecedented momentum following the landmark reforms, with private investment crossing $618.5 million, 105 authorisations issued to private entities, and a robust regulatory framework being strengthened to safeguard strategic and national security interests while promoting greater private participation, Space, science and technology minister Jitendra Singh informed the Rajya Sabha on Thursday.Replying to an unstarred question in the Rajya Sabha, Singh said cumulative private funding has expanded nearly six-fold in the last few years, rising from $100.5 million in 2021-22 to $348.5 million in 2023-24, before reaching $618.5 million by March 31, 2026, reflecting the growing confidence of domestic and global investors in India's rapidly evolving space ecosystem.Singh informed RS that 17 space startups have so far been authorised by IN-SPACe to undertake space activities, reflecting the emergence of a vibrant startup ecosystem that is increasingly contributing to India's space ambitions. Active space startups have increased from just one in 2014 to over 400 today, while private investment has crossed $600 million.Indian private companies have successfully demonstrated launch and orbital capabilities, placed more than 30 satellites into orbit, and flown nearly 45 payloads through platforms such as the PSLV Orbital Experimental Module (POEM), signalling the growing technological depth and commercial maturity of the country's private space sector.The earth observation public-private partnership has brought together leading Indian companies to develop the country's first privately owned Earth observation satellite constellation with a private investment commitment of around Rs 1,200 crore, demonstrating the growing confidence of industry in India's space reforms.Highlighting the govt's unwavering commitment to ensuring that rapid commercial growth is matched by robust national security safeguards, the minister informed the House that every application for private space activities is evaluated under IN-SPACe's norms, guidelines and procedures with due consideration to strategic, security and national interests before authorisation is granted. He further said IN-SPACe is preparing dedicated safety and security guidelines for space activities in India, in consultation with stakeholders, to further strengthen the country's regulatory architecture as private participation continues to expand.
Article Summary: India's space sector has witnessed unprecedented momentum following landmark reforms. Private investment has crossed $618.5 million, 105 authorisations issued to private entities. 17 space startups have so far been authorised by IN- SPACe to undertake space activities. Indian private companies have successfully demonstrated launch and orbital capabilities.

Article Title: Centre monitoring 2,485 Himalayan glacial lakes; 56 classified as 'very high risk'
Article Text:
Centre monitoring 2,485 Himalayan glacial lakes; 56 classified as 'very high risk (Image/AP)

(with inputs from PTI)

NEW DELHI: The Centre on Thursday said it is monitoring 2,485 glacial lakes spread across the Himalayan river basins using remote sensing techniques. It also informed Parliament that 56 glacial lakes have been classified as 'very high risk' following a scientific assessment conducted in 2025 due to their potential to trigger Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs).In a written reply in the Lok Sabha, jal shakti state minister Raj Bhushan Choudhary said the Central Water Commission (CWC) monitors glacial lakes larger than 10 hectares across four states and two Union Territories from June to October every year using remote sensing.The minister said the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA), in collaboration with national scientific agencies, carried out a scientific assessment of 189 high-risk glacial lakes across the Indian Himalayan Region in 2025."Based on the assessment, 56 glacial lakes were classified as very high risk considering their potential to trigger Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) and the exposure of downstream habitations and infrastructure," Choudhary said.He said the government has started establishing Glacial Lake Outburst Flood (GLOF) Early Warning Systems (EWS) at selected high-risk glacial lakes based on hazard assessments and continuous monitoring.However, the minister clarified that no end-to-end GLOF Early Warning System has been commissioned so far under the National GLOF Risk Mitigation Programme (NGRMP).Choudhary said the NDMA is implementing the NGRMP, which was approved in 2024 for Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Sikkim and Arunachal Pradesh at a cost of Rs 150 crore."The programme envisages Hazard and Risk Assessment, Monitoring and Early Warning Systems, site-specific structural and non-structural mitigation of measures, and community awareness and capacity building," he said.The minister added that Rs 52.50 crore has been allocated under the Monitoring and Early Warning System component of the programme.He also said the NDMA, along with the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC) and the Sikkim State Disaster Management Authority (SSDMA), carried out multi-agency expeditions to the high-risk South Lhonak and Shako Cho glacial lakes in Sikkim during 2023 and 2024. The expeditions were aimed at supporting scientific studies and developing monitoring systems, early warning mechanisms and mitigation measures.
Article Summary: The Centre said it is monitoring 2,485 glacial lakes spread across the Himalayan river basins using remote sensing techniques. It also informed Parliament that 56glacial lakes have been classified as'very high risk' due to their potential to trigger Glacial Lake Outburst Floods.

Article Title: India slams Pakistan's 'state-sponsored' misinformation at Asean, says Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance
Article Text:
India rejects Pakistan's ASEAN forum remarks​

NEW DELHI: India on Thursday categorically rejected Pakistan 's comments at the Asean Regional Forum in Manila, calling them "baseless and unwarranted".New Delhi slammed Islamabad of using the multilateral platform to peddle "state-sponsored disinformation" and divert attention from its record of sponsoring cross-border terrorism."It is no surprise that a multilateral forum has once again been cynically exploited by Pakistan to amplify falsehoods, peddle state-sponsored disinformation, and divert attention from its own well-documented record of sponsoring cross-border terrorism," MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said."The Union Territories of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh have been, are and will always remain integral and inalienable parts of India. Pakistan has no locus standi to comment on the same," MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said in response to media queries.Jaiswal also said India's position on the Indus Waters Treaty remained consistent, stating that the treaty stands in abeyance in response to Pakistan's continued sponsorship of cross-border terrorism, including the Pahalgam attack. "IWT will continue to be held in abeyance until Pakistan credibly and irrevocably abjures its support for cross-border terrorism," he said.Jaiswal also condemned Pakistan's practice of propagating "officially sponsored disinformation, often dressed in religious terminology such as the use of terms like 'Fitna al-Hindustan'.""This is a futile attempt by the Pakistani establishment to deflect attention from its own internal crises and its well-proven role as a safe haven for global terrorist networks," he said.He added that Pakistan would serve itself better by "dismantling the infrastructure of terrorism on its soil and putting its own house in order" instead of pointing fingers at its neighbours.External affairs minister S Jaishankar attended the foreign minister-level meetings under the Asean framework, including the Asean-India, East Asia Summit and Asean Regional Forum meetings in Manila over the last two days.Pakistan’s deputy prime minister and foreign minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar led Pakistan's delegation at the forum. He raised the Jammu and Kashmir dispute, calling for its resolution in accordance with UN Security Council resolutions, and expressed concern over India's decision to hold the Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance.
Article Summary: India categorically rejected Pakistan's comments at the Asean Regional Forum in Manila. New Delhi slammed Islamabad of using the multilateral platform to peddle "state-sponsored disinformation" and divert attention from its record of sponsoring cross-border terrorism. India's position on the Indus Waters Treaty remained consistent.

Article Title: Jaishankar says attacks on civilian shipping cannot be tolerated, calls for maritime security amid Middle East crisis
Article Text:
Addressing the summit, Jaishankar stressed the need for diplomatic engagement to resolve the ongoing crises in West Asia

Global conflicts affecting economy, Global South: Jaishankar

Holds bilateral meetings with Oman, UK, Netherlands, Philippines

Poll Vote & Share your view Should nations increase cooperation to combat cybercrime as urged by External Affairs Minister Jaishankar? Yes, it is essential for global security No, each nation should manage its own issues 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: External affairs minister S Jaishankar on Thursday said attacks on seafarers, commercial shipping and critical infrastructure "cannot be countenanced under any circumstances", as he called for strict adherence to international maritime law and greater regional cooperation during the 21st East Asia Summit (EAS) in Manila.Addressing the summit, Jaishankar stressed the need for diplomatic engagement to resolve the ongoing crises in Middle East and the Gulf region while reaffirming India's commitment to ensuring safe and unhindered navigation through international sea lanes."Under no circumstances can attacks on seafarers, civilian shipping or infrastructure be countenanced," Jaishankar said.He also reiterated India's support for Asean centrality and an open, free, inclusive and prosperous Indo-Pacific, saying the current global order is marked by uncertainty, strategic rivalry and growing geopolitical tensions.Following the summit, Jaishankar said in a post on X that the global economy was facing the consequences of multiple ongoing conflicts."The world economy is today reeling under the impact of multiple conflicts; energy, food and health security cannot be taken for granted, especially by the Global South," he said.He added that structural changes in global politics and technology were reshaping international relations."There are structural changes being created by new capabilities, exposure and relationships; politics and security increasingly override economics and efficiency while the propensity to take risks and push the envelope is growing," he said.On the South China Sea, Jaishankar reiterated India's support for the early conclusion of a substantive, effective and legally binding Code of Conduct (CoC) that is fully consistent with the 1982 United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) without prejudicing the legitimate rights of countries that are not parties to the negotiations.The minister also reaffirmed India's backing for Asean-led efforts to achieve a peaceful resolution to the crisis in Myanmar.Calling for stronger international cooperation against cybercrime, Jaishankar urged member countries to take coordinated action against transnational cyber scam networks that have trapped several Indian citizens in recent years, as per ANI.On the humanitarian front, he highlighted India's relief and rehabilitation assistance worldwide and said New Delhi had emerged as a leading donor to the United Nations Relief and Works Agency (UNRWA)."We believe that a two-state solution can lead to a lasting and durable peace," he said.Jaishankar also announced that India will host the 7th EAS Conference on Maritime Security Cooperation in Kochi and the EAS Maritime Heritage Festival at Lothal.On the sidelines of the Asean-related Foreign Ministers' Meetings, Jaishankar also held a series of bilateral meetings with leaders from Oman, the United Kingdom, the Netherlands, the Philippines and UNRWA.During his meeting with Oman's foreign minister Sayyid Badr bin Hamad Al Busaidi, the two leaders exchanged views on the latest developments in the Gulf region."Appreciated meeting with FM Sayyid Badr bin Hamad Al Busaidi of Oman in Manila. Exchanged perspectives on the recent developments in the Gulf region. Agreed to remain in touch," Jaishankar said in a post on X.In his first meeting with newly appointed UK foreign secretary Edward Miliband, Jaishankar congratulated him on his appointment and discussed expanding bilateral ties following the implementation of the recently concluded India-UK Free Trade Agreement."Congratulated him on his appointment. Agreed to build on the positive momentum generated through the recent India-UK FTA implementation, and work towards strengthening our Comprehensive Strategic Partnership," he posted.Jaishankar also met Christian Saunders, acting commissioner-general of UNRWA, and discussed India's humanitarian contributions, including commitments made at the recent donors' conference in the areas of health and capacity building.With Dutch foreign minister Tom Berendsen, Jaishankar reviewed the outcomes of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's recent visit to the Netherlands and discussed implementing the newly elevated India-Netherlands Strategic Partnership."The elevation of our ties to a Strategic Partnership has opened new avenues of cooperation, including in semiconductors, water resources, green hydrogen, health & culture," he said.The external affairs minister also called on Philippine President Ferdinand "Bongbong" Marcos Jr., conveying Prime Minister Narendra Modi's greetings and reaffirming India's support for the Philippines' Asean chairmanship."India-Philippines cooperation under the aegis of our Strategic Partnership continues to diversify as we implement the India-Philippines Plan of Action 2025-2029," Jaishankar said.Jaishankar is on a two-day visit to Manila, where he attended the Asean-India Foreign Ministers' Meeting, the East Asia Summit Foreign Ministers' Meeting, the Asean Regional Forum and the Quad Foreign Ministers' Meeting, where India, the United States, Japan and Australia reaffirmed their commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific and support for Asean centrality.
Article Summary: Jaishankar said attacks on seafarers, commercial shipping and critical infrastructure "cannot be countenanced under any circumstances" He called for strict adherence to international maritime law and greater regional cooperation during the 21st East Asia Summit (EAS) in Manila. He also reiterated India's support for Asean centrality and an open, free and inclusive Indo-Pacific.

Article Title: NEET deadlock washes out 4th straight day of Parliament monsoon session - key highlights
Article Text:
NEET deadlock washes out 4th straight day of Parliament monsoon session

Houses adjourned as NEET deadlock persists

NDA and opposition stage counter-protests

Meetings with Speaker fail to break impasse

Government urges debate, opposition sticks to demand

NEW DELHI: The monsoon session of Parliament remained deadlocked for a fourth straight day on Thursday as the ruling NDA and the opposition failed to break the impasse over the NEET paper leak controversy.Repeated disruptions in both Houses, protests inside and outside Parliament, and competing demands from the government and the opposition ensured that little legislative business could be conducted.The opposition continued to demand the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan before any discussion on the issue, while the government maintained it was ready for an immediate debate without preconditions.In the Lok Sabha, opposition MPs raised slogans and entered the well of the House after every adjournment, prompting repeated appeals from the Chair to maintain order.Speaker Om Birla urged members to allow Question Hour to continue, saying, "The Question Hour is the most important. After the Question Hour, the House is ready to discuss every issue. I will speak to the government and the government is also ready for discussions.I appeal to all members to participate in the discussion after the Question Hour."Parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju said the government was prepared for a discussion and even willing to let the opposition decide its timing and duration, but accused opposition parties of insisting on Pradhan's resignation instead of participating in the debate.The Rajya Sabha witnessed a similar impasse. Leader of opposition Mallikarjun Kharge was briefly allowed to speak after the House reassembled, but deputy chairman Harivansh stopped him, saying he had permitted the intervention only to help break the deadlock and begin the agreed discussion on NEET. Rijiju again maintained that the government was ready for a detailed debate, while accusing the Opposition of preferring protests over parliamentary proceedings.The standoff spilled outside Parliament as MPs from the INDIA bloc and the NDA held rival demonstrations near Makar Dwar.Opposition MPs, including Sonia Gandhi, Rahul Gandhi, Priyanka Gandhi Vadra and Akhilesh Yadav, demanded Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation, raising slogans such as "Isteefa do" and "gaddi chhodo." Meanwhile, NDA MPs accused the opposition of politicising the paper leak issue and avoiding a debate, responding with slogans including "sharam karo" and "Vande Mataram."Tensions briefly escalated when CPI(M) MP John Brittas was seen pulling a poster from BJP leader Arun Singh's hands, prompting Parliament security personnel to form a human chain to separate MPs from both sides.As disruptions continued, Union home minister Amit Shah met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla. Rahul Gandhi, Akhilesh Yadav, Saugata Roy and Mahua Moitra had also met the Speaker after Birla invited opposition leaders to discuss the schedule and format of a debate on NEET.Despite the meetings, Parliament remained paralysed through the day.Speaking to reporters, Rijiju urged opposition parties to participate in a discussion instead of stalling Parliament."If you want debate and discussion then you will play a positive role. If you are avoiding a parliamentary debate then you start putting conditions after conditions," ANI quoted him saying.Appealing for normal functioning of Parliament, he added, "We are with open heart asking and appealing the opposition party, don't stall the Parliament. There are important bills and very important business in this monsoon Session. We are ready to keep it aside and first of all initiate the discussion on the NEET exam paper leak and all the examinations which happen in the country. There are cases of paper leaks in different states. So it's a concern for everybody. It is not a concern for one party or one state because paper leaks have happened in so many places."The Congress, however, maintained that accountability must come first. Congress MP Gurjeet Singh Aujla said, "Such is the situation that neither farmers are happy nor the youth. The government has woken up now after there were so many paper leaks. LoP Rahul Gandhi is the shadow PM, but you don't let him speak. If you had listened to what he had said, this situation would not have arisen today. Education Minister must be asked to resign, and the government should apologise to the students. "With neither side willing to yield, the monsoon session completed its fourth consecutive day without substantive business, extending the deadlock over the NEET paper leak controversy.
Article Summary: The monsoon session of Parliament remained deadlocked for a fourth straight day on Thursday. The ruling NDA and the opposition failed to break the impasse over the NEET paper leak controversy. The opposition continued to demand the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan before any discussion. The government maintained it was ready for an immediate debate without preconditions.

Article Title: 30,000-ft health scare: British Airways launches probe after 3 pilots suffer food poisoning on Hyderabad-London flight
Article Text:
30,000-ft health scare: British Airways launches probe after 3 pilots suffer food poisoning on Hyderabad-London flight

NEW DELHI: Three British Airways pilots operating a flight from Hyderabad to London fell ill after suspected food poisoning, prompting the airline to launch an investigation into the incident. One of the pilots became incapacitated while the aircraft was cruising at 30,000 feet and required oxygen and urgent medical treatment after landing.The incident occurred on British Airways flight BA276, which departed Hyderabad for London Heathrow after the crew's overnight stopover in the city.According to Gulf News, the three pilots had breakfast together at a hotel lounge in Hyderabad before the flight. However, they reportedly consumed bottled water supplied by an external company.The first pilot became seriously unwell during the flight and was unable to continue normal duties. The other two pilots completed the journey safely, although their condition reportedly worsened as the flight progressed.The aircraft landed safely in London without any reported operational issues.British Airways has confirmed the incident and said it is reviewing the crew's accommodation arrangements in Hyderabad."The welfare of our colleagues is a top priority, and we are reviewing crew accommodation in this location following some isolated reports of sickness," the airline said in a statement.All three pilots have filed Air Safety Reports and were asked to provide medical samples to help doctors identify the source of the illness.The incident is considered highly unusual because commercial airlines follow strict safety protocols designed to prevent all members of the flight deck crew from becoming ill at the same time. These measures often include encouraging or requiring pilots to eat different meals.Food poisoning has long been recognised as a potential aviation safety risk and is one of the reasons airlines maintain strict crew meal policies.British Airways has faced similar incidents in the past. In 1984, a major food poisoning outbreak affected nearly 1,000 passengers, crew members and ground staff, disrupting the airline's global operations. More recently, in 2024, a United Airlines flight from Incheon to San Francisco returned to South Korea after several pilots and cabin crew members became ill shortly after take-off, according to Arabian Business.
Article Summary: Three British Airways pilots suffer food poisoning on flight from Hyderabad to London. One of the pilots became incapacitated while the aircraft was cruising at 30,000 feet. The aircraft landed safely in London without any reported operational issues. British Airways has confirmed the incident and said it is reviewing the crew's accommodation in Hyderabad.

Article Title: EU penalises Google €890m for breaking antitrust regulations
Article Text: The European Union (EU) on Thursday hit Google with a fine of ₹890 million after it said the technology behemoth broke digital antitrust regulations by setting up Google Play and its search engine to pull consumers towards its own services and apps to the detriment of competitors. It was the latest major crackdown on Big Tech by Brussels, which has led the world in reining in some of the world's largest companies from Silicon Valley to Beijing.It has done so despite the risk of incurring the wrath of President Donald Trump, who has lashed out at the 27-nation bloc's digital regulations amid a broader campaign against Europe. In the past, Trump has threatened retaliation if American tech companies are penalised.The European Commission, the bloc's executive branch, said that it was acting in the interest of consumers after an investigation of Google. "The best products should succeed because they're better, not because they're owned by the company running the search engine," said Teresa Ribera, the commission's executive vice president for clean, just and competitive transition.Google's president of global affairs Kent Walker blasted the fine as "product degradation driven by a small group of self-serving complainants" that will have a negative impact on European businesses and consumers.He said that the EU's Digital Markets Act forces Google "to strip away real-time search features Europeans love - like instant pricing and direct availability for hotels, flights, and restaurants - and dismantle safety protections on Google Play. "The EU describes the world's seven tech giants - Amazon, Apple, Google parent Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft and TikTok owner ByteDance - as "gatekeepers" that control access for consumers."In the EU, businesses have the right to compete fairly. Gatekeepers have the obligation to ensure a level playing field and consumers the right to choose for cheaper alternative offers," European Commission spokesperson Thomas Regnier said. AP
Article Summary: The European Commission said that it was acting in the interest of consumers after an investigation of Google. Google's president of global affairs Kent Walker blasted the fine as "product degradation driven by a small group of self- serving complainants" He said that the EU's Digital Markets Act forces Google "to strip away real-time search features Europeans love"

Article Title: Quote of the day by Che Guevara: 'The life of a single human being is worth millions of times more than all the property of the richest man on Earth' and how modern economic systems treat people like
Article Text:
'The life of a single human being is worth millions of times more than all the property of the richest man on earth'

"The life of a single human being is worth millions of times more than all the property of the richest man on earth".

The feeling that economic systems treat people like numbers on a spreadsheet.

A doctor’s speech in Havana

On Revolutionary Medicine

Placing people above property

uman beings have basic dignity that money cannot buy.

Focus on Property:

Focus on Life:

Real-world relevance today

Building a world that treats every human life as something beyond price.

When a single human life is weighed against material wealth, the right choice seems clear. Yet everyday real-world systems constantly do the math in reverse. Car companies weigh the cost of fixing a dangerous defect against the cost of paying out lawsuits for accidents. Investment firms buy up empty apartment buildings while unhoused people sleep on the streets outside. Modern society often measures value by what can be bought, sold, or protected behind gates.Against this financial way of thinking stands a bold statement:This quote rejects the idea of putting a price tag on people. It says that human life is so valuable that comparing it to money or property is a major moral mistake. The statement still matters today because it speaks to a common frustration:These words were spoken by Ernesto "Che" Guevara in Havana, Cuba, on August 19, 1960. Before he became a famous guerrilla leader in the Cuban Revolution, Guevara was a trained medical doctor.Guevara gave this speech, titled, to health workers and militia members. Cuba was undergoing massive political changes, and its healthcare system was struggling after hundreds of private doctors left the country.Guevara spoke to the remaining medical staff as a fellow doctor. He recalled his younger days traveling across South America as a medical student. On those trips, he saw poor families, untreated diseases, and children dying simply because their parents could not afford basic medicine.He explained how his experiences in the mountains during the revolution changed his thinking. Seeing poor farmworkers suffer from preventable illnesses taught him that doctoring could not just be about making money. He argued that a doctor’s real duty was to help build a society where no person's life was treated as worthless. The quote was not just an abstract idea; it was a clear instruction to health workers rebuilding a country's medical system.At its heart, the quote pushes a simple moral idea: HThe 18th-century philosopher Immanuel Kant famously wrote that everything in the world has either a price or a dignity. Things with a price can be replaced by money, but human beings have dignity, which cannot be replaced by anything.Guevara took this philosophical point and applied it to real-world politics and economics. The quote highlights two very different ways of viewing the world:Measures value through money, ownership, and land titles.Argues that human life and health carry an absolute value that no amount of physical property can equal.By placing one single life above "all the property of the richest man on earth," the quote sets up a clear rule. It insists that property rights are man-made laws meant to serve people. The moment property rules harm human health or survival, those rules lose their moral authority.The conflict between property rights and human survival remains a major issue across modern society.Look at global healthcare. During health crises, big drug companies closely guard their patents and formulas to protect their profits. Lower-income countries often face severe shortages of life-saving medicine because making cheaper versions is blocked by international trade laws. In these cases, financial ownership is given priority over human survival.Look also at housing in major cities. Local governments frequently deal with severe homelessness while thousands of investor-owned luxury apartments sit completely empty. When laws protect empty property assets while making it illegal for homeless people to sleep on public park benches, society acts as if property claims matter more than basic human shelter.The same principle applies to technology and corporate management. Social media companies often weigh user safety against corporate profit. They decide how much effort to spend on protecting users based on how it affects their earnings. When a platform chooses to design algorithms that keep people addicted at the expense of their mental health, it makes the exact choice Guevara spoke against.Putting this quote into practice means changing how institutions run. It requires companies, courts, and governments to make decisions where human safety and health always come first, no matter the financial cost.In 1960, Cuba used Guevara's ideas to build a free public healthcare system. Despite severe economic troubles and shortages, the nation prioritized free medical care and sent doctors to help during disasters around the world. It was a direct attempt to put these words into real practice.Whether in public health, city planning, or business, the core lesson remains unchanged:
Article Summary: Ernesto "Che" Guevara gave a speech in Havana, Cuba, on August 19, 1960. He argued that a doctor’s real duty was to help build a society where no person's life was treated as worthless. The statement still matters today because it speaks to a common frustration.

Article Title: Iran war risk: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon warns of oil shocks, sticky inflation and higher interest rates
Article Text: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has warned that the ongoing war in Iran could trigger oil and commodity price shocks, keeping inflation elevated and pushing interest rates higher than current market expectations, Reuters reported.The warning came in his annual letter to shareholders, a day after US President Donald Trump escalated pressure on Iran by threatening to target key infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened."Now, because of the war in Iran, we additionally face the potential for significant ongoing oil and commodity price shocks, along with the reshaping of global supply chains, which may lead to stickier inflation and ultimately higher interest rates than markets currently expect," Dimon said, news agency Reuters quoted as saying.Dimon, who has led JPMorgan for two decades, also highlighted broader geopolitical risks, including the war in Ukraine, tensions in the Middle East and friction with China."The challenges we all face are significant," he added.He said it remains uncertain whether the Iran war will achieve US objectives, while warning that nuclear proliferation remains the biggest risk linked to Iran.Markets have already begun pricing in these risks, with expectations of interest rate cuts this year largely fading amid rising inflation concerns triggered by the conflict.Last week, the benchmark S&P 500 index recorded its worst quarterly performance since 2022, weighed down by rising energy prices and geopolitical uncertainty since late February.Dimon noted that the US economy remains resilient, with consumers continuing to earn and spend, and businesses staying broadly healthy, though signs of weakening have emerged.He cautioned that economic strength has been supported by significant government deficit spending and past stimulus, while infrastructure investment needs continue to grow.At the same time, he pointed to positives such as fiscal stimulus under President Trump's "Big, Beautiful Bill", deregulation policies and rising capital expenditure driven by artificial intelligence.On financial stability, Dimon said the $1.8-trillion private credit market "probably" does not pose a systemic risk, despite investor concerns and recent withdrawals from such funds.However, he warned that in a downturn, losses across leveraged lending could exceed expectations as credit standards have weakened.Private credit markets also lack transparency and rigorous valuation benchmarks, increasing the risk of investor exits if conditions worsen, he said.Separately, Dimon criticised revised US capital rules, including Basel III and GSIB surcharge norms, calling aspects of the proposals "nonsensical" and "very flawed".He said JPMorgan’s GSIB surcharge would fall only to 5.0%, a level he described as “absurd” and “un-American”, arguing it penalises the bank’s scale and performance.
Article Summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon warns Iran war could trigger oil and commodity price shocks. He also highlighted broader geopolitical risks, including the war in Ukraine, tensions in the Middle East and friction with China. Dimon criticised revised US capital rules, including Basel III and GSIB surcharge norms, calling aspects of the proposals "nonsensical"

Article Title: How selling Alaska in 1867 was a costly mistake for Russia
Article Text:
Image: Wikipedia

Why Russia agreed to the Alaska purchase

The hidden wealth Russia gave away

Strategic and geopolitical consequences







In 1867, Russia’s decision to sell Alaska to America turned out to be a historic mistake that was to go unnoticed for years to come. The sum paid by the US was only $7,200,000. Although, in the light of the circumstances at the time, it appeared logical, retrospectively, the act looks extremely shortsighted. Russia found itself economically constrained, geographically far away from the region and fearful of its falling into the hands of the British without any compensation. In reality, however, the region had proved immensely valuable both strategically and naturally. Its vast reserves of oil, gold, and natural resources would later transform it into one of the most economically significant territories in the United States.The choice of selling Alaska was based on economic and political reasons. By the middle of the 19th century, the Russian Empire experienced financial difficulties as a result of the expensive Crimean War. It had become increasingly hard for the country to manage such remote and thinly populated areas as Alaska.According to EBSCO , “the Russian-American Company was in decline, and the colony failed to yield any profit”.In addition to this, the lucrative fur trade had declined, making the territory much less economically valuable for the empire.At the same time, Russia was afraid that Britain might capture Alaska in case another war broke out between the two countries. Selling the colony to the friendly United States appeared as a logical step. As the Office of the Historian states, William H. Seward, U.S. Secretary of State, “it was a chance to expand its influence in North America and hinder further growth of Britain”.For Russia, the transaction was a chance to minimise the losses. However, it greatly undervalued the potential of Alaska.Another thing that Russia did not expect was the amount of riches that Alaska possessed. Within several decades after the acquisition, the state saw discoveries of enormous deposits of gold, oil, and other minerals.“Alaska has produced more than 40 million ounces of gold,” according to the US Geological Survey. Furthermore, Alaska is home to some of the world's most significant undeveloped mineral deposits, according to the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) . But even more importantly, the state's oil deposits turned out to be extremely valuable. The Prudhoe Bay oil field was discovered in 1968 and became one of North America's biggest.In retrospect, the amount paid by the Americans, $7.2 million, or two cents per acre, was rather laughable. According to Howard I. Kushner , the deal “robbed Russia of an enormously rich territory, the true value of which would only be recognised in the twentieth century.”In addition to resources, there is the question of strategy and how important Alaska is to the United States from a strategic standpoint. This is a very good place when it comes to defence strategy. It borders on the Arctic Ocean and also happens to be close to Russia.During the period of the Cold War, the Alaskan region became a frontier area for the United States, becoming home to military facilities and warning systems.According to Col. Michael J. Forsyth, U.S. Army, the closeness of Russia and Alaska, only about ninety kilometres apart across the Bering Strait, meant that this region became highly significant to the United States' defence plans.From today's point of view, the strategic location of Alaska makes the state very important to the policies of the U.S. related to the Arctic, energy security, and even environmental policies.Thinking back, what seems clear to us now is that the Russians sold Alaska due to immediate needs rather than future considerations. In order to solve the problem, they lost a valuable resource for Russia.Conclusively, the Alaska Purchase should not have been done as it has had a significant impact that will remain throughout history.
Article Summary: Russia sold Alaska to the U.S. in 1867 for only $7,200,000. The sale was based on economic and political reasons. However, the region proved immensely valuable both strategically and naturally. Alaska is home to some of the world's most significant undeveloped mineral deposits. The Prudhoe Bay oil field was discovered in 1968.

Article Title: AI data centers are causing 'stress' not just to tech companies, but also private insurers
Article Text:
Massive investments, growing risks



Unique challenges for data centre insurance companies





AI data centers are reportedly putting new pressure on the insurance industry. According to a CNBC report, experts warn that the rapid growth of these projects is becoming a “stress test” not just for tech companies, but also for private insurers and lenders. Consulting firm McKinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. As costs rise, tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects. The scale of investments is creating challenges for insurers.“When you put $10 to $20 billion plus in a single location, it creates capacity issues in the marketplace,” said Tom Harper, data center leader at insurance broker Gallagher as quoted in the CNBC report.He added that the sector has been a “real stress test” over the last few years, as insurers struggle to provide coverage for such large and complex projects. While these facilities are seen as high-quality assets, Harper said insuring them has become difficult due to their size and risk concentration.Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Companies including Nvidia, Microsoft and BlackRock have already taken part in large investment deals.Rajat Rana, partner at Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, described the scale as unprecedented. “We’re talking about trillions of dollars, and almost going back to the same cycle where there’s almost no transparency about the financing structures — the scale is astronomical,” he said. He added that the boom in AI data centers could be “the largest peacetime investment project in human history.”Experts say the way these projects are financed is adding another layer of risk. Much of the funding is happening through complex and less transparent debt structures. Rana warned that this trend feels like “deja vu,” referring to past financial cycles. He said the lack of transparency could create risks for investors, insurers and pension funds.As stated in the CNBC report, data centers offer unique challenges due to their mix of real estate and advanced technology. Insurers are now designing custom policies to manage these risks. However, challenges remain. Large facilities are often located in areas prone to extreme weather, and they store expensive equipment, sometimes even before installation.Another key concern is the mismatch between the lifespan of data centers and the hardware they use. While data centers can last decades, GPUs — the chips powering AI — typically last around seven years. Rana described this as a “GPU debt treadmill,” saying, “This is almost like a treadmill that these AI data centers are running on.”He explained that companies may need to keep raising debt to upgrade infrastructure as technology evolves.
Article Summary: Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. McKinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. Tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects.

Article Title: After DU, JNU asks students to stay away from CJP protest, warns disciplinary action
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) on Friday advised its students and staff to refrain from participating in the protest at Jantar Mantar and cautioned them against "irresponsible social media activity", warning that violations could invite legal and disciplinary action.In an advisory posted on its official X handle, JNU said:"All stakeholders of JNU's epistemic community are advised to act responsibly and prioritize their personal safety. In accordance with the Hon'ble Supreme Court of India's directions on public demonstrations, you are requested to refrain from participating in or visiting gatherings at or around Jantar Mantar, New Delhi.Please exercise responsibility on social media. Violations may invite legal consequences under applicable laws as well as disciplinary action under the University's code of conduct.You are also encouraged to uphold the values of academic responsibility and responsible citizenship."The advisory comes as students from universities across India have been participating in large numbers in the CJP-led protest at Jantar Mantar, where demonstrators have been demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.Over the past few days, several universities and higher education bodies have issued similar advisories.Delhi University had urged students to stay away from the protest and exercise restraint on social media, while the Association of Indian Universities (AIU) had written to vice-chancellors of around 1,170 member universities asking them to counsel students against participating in demonstrations.The latest advisory also comes amid visible differences within the Delhi University community.While several DU colleges have shared posts from their official social media handles expressing support for Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, student unions and societies in a number of colleges have issued statements condemning the police action on protesters and reiterating the demand for the minister's resignation.
Article Summary: Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) advises its students and staff to refrain from participating in the protest at Jantar Mantar. The advisory comes as students from universities across India have been participating in large numbers in the CJP-led protest. Protesters have been demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

Article Title: CJP protest: Central Delhi goes into lockdown mode, mobile internet suspended near Jantar Mantar
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Central Delhi went into lockdown Thursday evening, with mobile internet services being suspended in the 1.5 kilometre radius of Jantar Mantar and traders in Connaught Place shutting the market down at 6.30 pm.The usually bustling market in the heart of Delhi bore a deserted look at 7 pm with downed shutters and vacant parking lots. The Rajiv Chowk Metro station, alongside many others, was shut at 7.30 am, for the second day in a row.Crowds started thinning and traders packed up as Thursday wore on, following an advisory issued by a traders’ organisation asking shops, offices and restaurants to shut by 6.30 pm as a precaution over ongoing Cockroach Janta Party protests.The advisory, issued by NDTA president Atul Bhargava following directions conveyed by NDMC chairperson and vice-chairperson, urged traders to cooperate “to avoid any unpleasant situation and prevent loss of property or injury”.“There was a huge gathering in CP, with thousands of people coming to the area. Our advisory kept in mind the safety of our staff, customers and businesses,” Bhargava told TOI. An NDMC official, too, said the advisory was issued “only from a safety point of view to avoid any untoward incident”.On top of that, the blocking of mobile internet in Delhi’s commercial nerve centre crippled digital payment systems at its parking facilities and inconvenienced commuters who heavily depend on digital payment platforms while using cabs and autos.The internet disruption forced attendants at several parking lots to temporarily switch to cash-only payments. With payment machines not working, parking operators began asking motorists whether they were carrying cash before allowing them to enter, while those relying solely on UPI or cards were turned away. “We are discouraging vehicles from entering the parking lots until the network is restored,” an operator said.“These days almost everyone uses UPI. I hardly carry cash. I only had a Rs 500 note, and the parking staff said they may not have change. It became very inconvenient,” said Aman Vats, a resident of Saket who drove to CP.Another person said he had to leave his vehicle parked temporarily and walk around searching for an ATM to withdraw cash before he could pay the parking charges in CP’s Inner Circle.Commuters also reported long delays and difficulties in reaching hospitals, offices and other destinations.The mobile internet shutdown compounded the problem commuters faced with several metro stations in central Delhi area already closed. Dharmesh Singh, who was travelling to Lady Hardinge Hospital for a scheduled checkup, said, “I couldn’t even book a cab or a bike taxi from CP because the internet wasn’t working,” a commuter said.Cab aggregators also struggled as app-based services were affected. “We can somehow pick up passengers, but ending the ride is difficult because the app needs internet. Payments are another issue. Most people pay online, but with no internet neither they nor we can complete the transaction,” said a rider with a cab aggregator platform.Auto-rickshaw drivers said many commuters were forced to look for cash as UPI payments failed. “Passengers kept asking where they could find an ATM,” said a driver parked near CP.
Article Summary: Traders in Connaught Place shut the market down at 6.30 pm. The usually bustling market bore a deserted look at 7 pm with downed shutters and vacant parking lots. The Rajiv Chowk Metro station, alongside many others, was shut at 7.30 am.

Article Title: Delhi schools shift to hybrid classes, tweak transport amid CJP protest
Article Text:
Schools in central Delhi are altering class schedules and transport arrangements (AI image used for representational purpose only).





NEW DELHI: Schools in central Delhi have begun altering class schedules and transport arrangements as traffic restrictions and Delhi Metro curbs continue amid the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest over the alleged irregularities in the NEET-UG examination.R S Junior Modern School on Humayun Road has informed parents that Nursery to Class 5 students will attend classes in the hybrid mode — both physical and online — from July 24 until further notice, citing the "ongoing situation in Central Delhi".Students attending classes from home have been asked to follow the regular timetable and join lessons through Microsoft Teams while wearing the school uniform, according to a circular.The school said its buses would continue to operate as per the regular schedule.Meanwhile, Sardar Patel Vidyalaya in Lodhi Estate, which held online classes on Tuesday and Wednesday, has resumed regular offline classes from Thursday.The school said its buses would ply on their usual routes and adhere to the regular schedule. However, it advised parents of students living in areas affected by traffic restrictions to coordinate with the school transport staff and collect their wards either from the campus or designated points ahead, as buses may be diverted for safety reasons.The changes come as several roads in central Delhi remain affected due to heightened security arrangements around the protest site, while repeated closures of multiple Delhi Metro stations have disrupted commuting, causing traffic congestion and delays.Separately, the Diocese of Delhi of the Church of North India issued a statement expressing solidarity with students and families affected by the alleged irregularities in public examinations.The Diocese called for a thorough, impartial and transparent investigation into the alleged irregularities, urging accountability be fixed and action taken against those found responsible.
