It also stressed the need for institutional reforms to safeguard the integrity and credibility of public examinations.It said every student deserves an education system founded on honesty, fairness, transparency and equal opportunity, and appealed to the authorities to act with urgency while ensuring justice and restoring public confidence in the examination process.
Article Summary: Schools in central Delhi have begun altering class schedules and transport arrangements. The changes come amid the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest over the alleged irregularities in the NEET-UG examination. R S Junior Modern School has informed parents that Nursery to Class 5 students will attend classes in the hybrid mode.

Article Title: Valuables worth Rs 2.7 crore stolen over five years from Sachin Ahir's home in Mumbai
Article Text:
Valuables worth Rs 2.65 crore were stolen from Sachin Ahir's Worli residence over five years (File Photo).





MUMBAI: Valuables worth Rs 2.65 crore have been stolen from the Worli residence of legislative council deputy speaker Sachin Ahir of the Shiv Sena over the past five years, his son Krishna has said in a complaint to the police.The stolen items include a 100-gram gold biscuit, diamond-studded gold bangles, a diamond-studded gold band, five diamond necklaces, diamond rings, silver glasses, silver utensils, including jugs, plates and lotas, along with an unspecified amount of cash.Krishna claimed that after a fire caused by an alleged short circuit damaged the residence on October 13, 2025, the family initially believed that cash withdrawn for household expenses had been destroyed in the blaze. Later, reviewing the CCTV footage, they discovered that portions of the recordings were missing, raising suspicion.Krishna said his four domestic help systematically stole cash, gold jewellery and other valuables worth around Rs 2.65 crore in the past five years. The city crime branch has taken over the probe.Krishna said that though he has been residing at a flat in Khar for the past two years due to work commitments in the film industry, he frequently visited the family's residence at Gulrukh Building, Worli seaface, where his parents and sister continue to live.
Article Summary: Valuables worth Rs 2.65 crore stolen from Sachin Ahir's Worli residence over five years. Items include a 100-gram gold biscuit, diamond-studded gold bangles, five diamond necklaces, diamond rings, silver glasses, silver utensils, including jugs, plates and lotas. City crime branch has taken over the probe.

Article Title: Mumbai University rolls back admission ban on 68 law, BEd colleges
Article Text:
Mumbai University had taken action against several such colleges last year as well





Mumbai: The University of Mumbai has withdrawn its decision to bar admissions at 68 law and BEd colleges that failed to appoint university-approved principals and teachers.The colleges will now be allowed to admit students to 50% of their sanctioned intake during the current academic year, subject to a penalty of Rs 10 lakh each.The university had earlier decided not to grant affiliation to 39 law colleges and 29 BEd colleges, effectively keeping them out of the state CET Cell’s centralised admission process. The institutions subsequently sought a review of the decision.Under the revised action, 11 law colleges and 23 BEd colleges where fewer than half the teachers have university approval will face a Rs 5 lakh penalty. Their intake will be reduced by 25%.Colleges where more than 50% of the faculty have been approved will be fined Rs 2 lakh each, but will face no restriction on admissions.The action follows repeated concerns over the absence of approved teachers, principals, adequate infrastructure and academic standards at several law and teacher-training colleges.The number of law colleges in the state has grown rapidly following increased demand for legal education.A similar expansion took place in the B.Ed sector when teacher-training courses attracted large numbers of students. However, faculty recruitment and infrastructure have not kept pace with the rise in institutions. Mumbai University had taken action against several such colleges last year as well. At the time, the state govt asked the university to give the institutions six months to address the deficiencies and permit them to participate in admissions. The university later granted affiliation after imposing a penalty of Rs 1 lakh.As the colleges failed to comply with the requirements even this year, the university initially ordered a complete admission ban. The decision was, however, reversed within days.A senior MU official said that the decision to relax colleges without approved principals and teachers would be permitted to fill only half their seats and would have to comply with all prescribed norms within four months
Article Summary: University of Mumbai has withdrawn its decision to bar admissions at 68 law and BEd colleges that failed to appoint university-approved principals and teachers. The colleges will now be allowed to admit students to 50% of their sanctioned intake during the current academic year, subject to a penalty of Rs 10 lakh each.

Article Title: Action against Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation official over fire certificates
Article Text:
Navi Mumbai:





The Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation general body on Wednesday passed a resolution and took action against the assistant director of town planning, Somnath Kekan, over his administrative decisions. Kekan had allegedly issued construction commencement certificate and no-objection certificate for fire on the same day without following the due procedure. Kekan allegedly granted permissions when various violations related to the building were sub judice.
Article Summary: The Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation general body passed a resolution and took action against the assistant director of town planning. Kekan had allegedly issued construction commencement certificate and no-objection certificate for fire on the same day. He allegedly granted permissions when various violations related to the building were sub judice.

Article Title: SIAM urges state govt to avoid punitive action against seed firms without expert reports
Article Text:
Seed germination and crop growth depend on much more than just seed quality. Factors such as rainfall, soil moisture, sowing timing, soil type, and field management play critical roles

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar





:The Seed Industries Association of Maharashtra (SIAM) has demanded that all farmer complaints be thoroughly, impartially, and scientifically investigated before any punitive action is taken following a state crackdown on alleged “bogus” soybean seeds,.SIAM director (technical) Ravindra Jagtap expressed concern over the trend of filing criminal cases against seed companies before scientific inquiries are completed.“Seed germination and crop growth depend on much more than just seed quality. Factors such as rainfall, soil moisture, sowing timing, soil type, and field management play critical roles,” Jagtap said. “Therefore, scientific evidence and a report from an expert committee are essential before determining fault.”The association argued that taking action against companies prior to a scientific investigation undermines the govt’s ‘ease of doing business’ principles.While acknowledging the seriousness of the issue, SIAM adviser Shaligram Wankhede stated, “Protecting the interests of farmers is the government’s paramount responsibility. However, it is a matter of concern that actions are being taken against seed companies before investigations are concluded.”Official data shows that by mid-July, the state agriculture department had received over 3,600 complaints related to soybean germination, leading to four criminal proceedings. Early analysis suggests these issues could impact approximately 6,000 hectares of land. However, SIAM pointed out that with over 41 lakh hectares under soybean cultivation in Maharashtra, the affected area is relatively negligible.In a representation to the state govt, SIAM, which represents 109 registered industries, emphasised that the seed industry is research-driven and requires a stable regulatory environment.“Developing new varieties requires years of effort and substantial investment. Harsh actions taken in the absence of scientific findings could adversely affect investment, research, and the development of new technologies,” the association stated, adding that such a stance could undermine the spirit of the Seeds Act, 1966.Reiterating its commitment to providing quality seeds and cooperating with legitimate inquiries, SIAM called for an environment that encourages innovation.“Farmers and the seed industry are mutually dependent. There is a pressing need for a science-based, fair, and balanced approach that protects both parties,” the association stated. “We hope Maharashtra will adopt a stance that serves as a model for the rest of the country.” State agriculture minister Dattatray Bharne could not be reached for comment.
Article Summary: Seed Industries Association of Maharashtra (SIAM) has demanded that all farmer complaints be thoroughly, impartially, and scientifically investigated before any punitive action is taken. The state agriculture department had received over 3,600 complaints related to soybean germination, leading to four criminal proceedings.

Article Title: HC asks NTA to produce original NEET answer sheets in petitions alleging score discrepancies
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





The Aurangabad bench of the Bombay high court on Thursday directed the National Testing Agency to produce the original answer sheets of three NEET (UG) 2026 aspirants who have challenged their results, alleging significant discrepancies between their OMR responses and the marks reflected in their scorecards.A division bench of Justices Nitin Suryawanshi and Abasaheb Shinde issued notices to the respondents and directed the National Testing Agency (NTA) to place the original records before the court on the next hearing date, July 30.Deputy Solicitor General of India Ajay Talhar, who appeared in one of the matters, said, “The HC has issued notices and asked the NTA to be ready with the original records of these students by next date, which is July 30.”The petitions have been filed by Hricha Prafull Deshpande of Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Soham Nitin Gavte of Beed district and Atharva Jagtap of Dhule district. All three students have alleged that the marks declared by the NTA do not match the scores they calculated after comparing their OMR sheets with the official answer key and have sought verification of their original answer sheets.Appearing for Hricha Deshpande, advocate Amit Yadkikar, assisted by advocates Sneha Yadkikar and Prasanna Jarare, submitted that the 17-year-old candidate accessed her scanned OMR sheet on the NTA portal on July 13 and found it consistent with her performance in the June 21 re-test.However, an OMR sheet emailed by the NTA on July 15 allegedly differed from the version available on the portal. Despite lodging a grievance before the declaration of results, the NTA allegedly did not respond and declared the result on July 16 based on the disputed record. The petition seeks production of the original physical OMR sheet and correction of the result based on the authentic record.Advocates Sudarshan Salunke and Mahendra Gandle, appearing for Soham Gavte, told the court that his OMR sheet and the official answer key indicated he should have secured 522 marks, but his scorecard reflected only 95 marks. They said the student repeatedly emailed the NTA between July 17 and 19 seeking rectification but received no response.Appearing for Atharva Jagtap, advocate Narendra Sonawane submitted that the student’s OMR sheet, received by email on July 14, showed he was to get 597 marks. However, his scorecard displayed only 45 marks. The petition further claimed that even a calculation based on the published scorecard worked out to 61 marks, suggesting an apparent error. Despite approaching the NTA through its grievance mechanism, the issue remained unresolved, the petition stated.Advocate Rohit Sarvadnya, chief standing counsel for the Union of India, appeared in two of the petitions. The matter will now be heard on July 30, when the NTA is expected to produce the original records before the court.
Article Summary: Three students allege significant discrepancies between their OMR responses and the marks reflected in their scorecards. Petition seeks production of the original physical OMR sheet and correction of the result based on the authentic record. The matter will now be heard on July 30, when the NTA is expected to produce the original records before the court.

Article Title: Widespread rain across Marathwada gives new lease of life to kharif crops
Article Text:
Rain in Nanded

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





After a considerable gap, Marathwada received widespread showers with the region recording 19 mm rainfall between Tuesday and Wednesday morning.The revival of monsoon in the region has relieved the farming community as many farmers were staring at the crisis of resowing for the kharif season. Among the eight districts of Marathwada, Hingoli received the maximum 39 mm rainfall, followed by Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar (29 mm), Nanded ( 22 mm), Jalna ( 21 mm), Parbhani (16 mm), Dharashiv, Beed (both 10 mm) and Latur (9 mm).Many parts of Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar city also reported occasional spells of light to moderate showers till Wednesday evening with 14 mm rainfall getting recorded between 8.30am and 6.30pm, as per IMD data. Ravi Kale , a farmer from Jatwada area of Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, said kharif crops were under stress until the latest showers. “While many other parts of the state were witnessing heavy showers, the rainy season was weak especially in July. There was a delay in sowing and a gap in the rainfall activity had posed worries before us. We pray for good rains in the days to come,” he said.Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar division has reported 79% sowing for kharif season by July 20 whereas Latur division has recorded 77% sowing. As per the India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecast, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar and surrounding areas of the region are likely to have generally cloudy skies with chances of moderate rain for at least next 48-hours.Marathwada has received 205mm rainfall since June 1, which is 77% of the expected showers.
Article Summary: Marathwada has received 205mm rainfall since June 1, which is 77% of the expected showers. Hingoli received the maximum 39 mm rainfall, followed by Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar (29 mm), Nanded ( 22 mm), Jalna ( 21 mm), Parbhani (16 mm), Dharashiv, Beed (both 10 mm) and Latur (9 mm)

Article Title: Railway minister silent on funding & timeline for Dharashiv-Sambhajinagar rail route
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





The proposed 240km railway line linking Dharashiv, Beed and Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar continues to remain in limbo, with Union railway minister Ashwini Vaishnaw reiterating that only survey work for the project has been sanctioned. He provided no clarity on funding or timelines.Responding to a question raised by Beed NCP (SP) MP Bajrang Sonawane in the Lok Sabha, Vaishnaw said the detailed project report (DPR) for the rail route is being prepared following the sanction of survey work.Sonawane had sought details on the release of initial funds, raised concerns over inadequate budgetary allocations and asked whether the project would receive funding in the forthcoming Union Budget.In his written reply on Wednesday, Vaishnaw did not address the funding issue directly, stating that the project's future would depend on statutory approvals and consultations.“After preparation of DPR, sanctioning of the project requires consultation with various stakeholders, including state govts and necessary approvals, viz. appraisal of NITI Aayog, Ministry of Finance etc. As sanctioning of projects is a continuous and dynamic process, exact timelines depend upon appraisals and approvals by various stakeholders,” he said.The Railway Board had sanctioned the final location survey for the Dharashiv-Beed-Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar rail route in March last year. The survey is intended to determine the final alignment of the proposed railway line and facilitate preparation of the DPR.The lack of a definitive commitment has disappointed rail activists, who have long been advocating the project. “The rail line in question has been the dream for Marathwada for more than two decades. Now with Beed getting on the rail map of India finally, the project has become more relevant and necessary,” said railway activist Raj Somani.
Article Summary: The proposed 240km railway line linking Dharashiv, Beed and Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar continues to remain in limbo. Union railway minister Ashwini Vaishnaw reiterating that only survey work for the project has been sanctioned. He provided no clarity on funding or timelines.

Article Title: Artist’s oppose privatization of Keshavrao Bhosale Natyagruh
Article Text:
Kolhapur

:

TOI





Theatre artists from across Kolhapur submitted a written statement to district collector Dr Vijay Rathod, requesting state govt to take custody of Keshavrao Bhosale Natyagruh again after the Kolhapur Municipal Corporation (KMC) decided to privatise the palace theatre by handing its management to a private company.The theatre is owned by the state govt. After the municipal corporation came into existence, the govt transferred the theatre to the KMC for management.Actor Anand Kale, while talking to, said, “A theatre or cultural activity is a basic need of the city and it should be run on the principle of no profit, no loss. It is from that role that the govt transferred the theater to the municipal corporation. However, the KMC has violated the rules as the proposal to hand over the management of the theatre to a private institution has been approved in the general body meeting of KMC held on July 20.”The reconstruction of the historic theatre, which was completely gutted in a massive fire in Aug 2024, is in its final stages. Against this backdrop, a review meeting was held on Thursday at the Govt Rest House and it was attended by MLA Rajesh Kshirsagar, KMC Mayor Rouparani Nikam, KMC Commissioner Dr. Rajendra Bharud, other KMC officials, contractors and theatre artists.After both sides, the civic administration and theatre artists, clarified their positions, Kshirsagar directed the municipal commissioner and mayor to reconsider the matter in the next General Body meeting.Speaking on the occasion, Kshirsagar said, “As the city’s elected representative, I assure everyone that no injustice will be done to the artists, no financial burden will fall upon them and the art itself suffers no harm. An immediate allocation of Rs1.80 crore from own funds for the installation of the final DP (Distribution Transformer) unit to expedite completion has been made. We are seeking for inauguration of Keshavrao Bhosale Natyagruh, musical fountain at Rankala lake, Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Memorial, and the flood control project during the chief minister’s visit on Aug 9 to Kolhapur. ”While the reconstruction work of the theatre is towards completion, the contractor appointed to carry out works like grouting, roofing and removal of cracks from the walls has been removed owing to dissatisfactory work.
Article Summary: The theatre is owned by the state govt. After the municipal corporation came into existence, the govt transferred the theatre to the KMC for management. The reconstruction of the historic theatre, which was completely gutted in a massive fire in Aug 2024, is in its final stages.

Article Title: 35,000-seat cricket stadium planned near Kolhapur
Article Text:
Located about 15 km from Kolhapur city, the proposed complex will be developed as a multi-sport hub rather than a cricket-only venue.

Kolhapur

:

A 12.76-hectare plot at Vikaswadi MIDC near Kagal has been transferred by the Maharashtra government to the Kolhapur District Cricket Association (KDCA) for the development of an international cricket stadium.The memorandum of understanding (MoU) was signed on Tuesday between the state sports department and the KDCA. Former Rajya Sabha MP and KDCA president Sambhajiraje Chhatrapati signed the agreement on behalf of the association, which will now be responsible for raising funds and executing the project.Located about 15 km from Kolhapur city, the proposed complex will be developed as a multi-sport hub rather than a cricket-only venue. Besides the stadium, it will include facilities for boxing, judo, archery, wrestling, swimming, table tennis, fencing and other sports. A cricket museum will also be built, with the project expected to generate employment opportunities for local residents.The cricket stadium, with a seating capacity of around 35,000, will be built in accordance with BCCI and ICC norms. The project will feature a modern pavilion, practice grounds, an indoor training centre, a sports science centre, a clubhouse, floodlights, a cricket museum, parking facilities for players, officials and spectators, internal roads, drainage systems, a solar power project and advanced security infrastructure.The demand for an international cricket stadium in Kolhapur had been pursued for years by veteran cricketers Bal Patankar and Ruturaj Ingale, who sought land for the project. In September 2024, Sambhajiraje Chhatrapati took up the proposal with then deputy chief minister Ajit Pawar , following which the government approved the transfer of land from MIDC to the KDCA.The MoU was signed on Tuesday, on the eve of Ajit Pawar’s birth anniversary, in the presence of chief minister Devendra Fadnavis and deputy chief minister Sunetra Pawar.Sambhajiraje said construction of the stadium would begin soon and that the facility would be built to international standards with an eye on the future of the sport.“The stadium will create new opportunities for thousands of aspiring cricketers in western Maharashtra and the Konkan region. Hosting national and international cricket competitions, providing high-quality training facilities, and promoting sports tourism will boost the local economy significantly. By raising the necessary funds and completing all processes transparently and with proper planning, we are determined to build a world-class cricket stadium for Kolhapur as soon as possible,” he said.Ministers Prakash Abitkar, Hasan Mushrif and Shivendraraje Bhosale, along with KDCA office-bearers, were present at the MoU signing ceremony in Mumbai.
Article Summary: A 12.76-hectare plot at Vikaswadi MIDC near Kagal has been transferred by the Maharashtra government to the Kolhapur District Cricket Association. Besides the stadium, it will include facilities for boxing, judo, archery, wrestling, swimming, table tennis, fencing and other sports.

Article Title: Navi Mumbai ACB arrests MSEDCL assistant engineer in Rs 50,000 bribery case
Article Text:
The Navi Mumbai ACB has made a crucial arrest, capturing an assistant engineer for demanding a bribe (Representative image)





NAVI MUMBAI: The Navi Mumbai Anti Corruption Bureau (ACB) team laid a trap Thursday afternoon at MSEDCL's Taloja office and caught red-handed an assistant engineer while accepting a bribe of Rs 50,000 from the complainant for providing feasibility report for installation of new electricity meter and transformer at an Electrical company in Taloja MIDC area.Dharmaraj Sonake, Deputy Superintendent (Dy SP) of Navi Mumbai ACB informed that, the arrested accused has been identified as Rohit Revanand More (32), a class 2 officer.An FIR under section 7 of Prevention of Corruption Act was registered against the accused More at Taloja police station, for the offence of a public servant accepting gratification, a bribe, for performing official duty.The complainant is an electrical contractor for PN Electrical Company in Taloja MIDC area.The company has given him work order of installing MSEDCL's new electricity meter connection and transformer.The contractor had submitted an application to MSEDCL's Panvel division office in Taloja phase-1 on July 7 for seeking permission.Dy SP Sonake said, "Since the work of preparing the feasibility report was being handled by MSEDCL's assistant engineer Rohit More at Taloja office, the complainant met More on July 10 and inquired about the status of his application for seeking feasibility report of installation of new electricity meter and transformer at PN Electrical Company, when More demanded Rs 50,000 bribe for clearing his file.""Hence, on July 13, the contractor filed a complaint with the Navi Mumbai ACB office at AMPC market premises in Turbhe. On July 14, the complaint verification confirmed Rs 50,000 bribe was demanded by More.""Subsequently, on Thursday July 23 afternoon, a trap was laid for More at MSEDCL's Taloja office and he was caught red-handed while accepting the demanded bribe of Rs 50,000 from the complainant contractor. After arresting More, his mobile has been seized and further his house search will be conducted for finding any indiscriminatory documents related to amassing wealth disproportionate to his actual source of income. "
Article Summary: Navi Mumbai Anti Corruption Bureau (ACB) team laid a trap Thursday afternoon at MSEDCL's Taloja office. Assistant engineer was caught red-handed while accepting a bribe of Rs 50,000. Accused has been identified as Rohit Revanand More (32), a class 2 officer.

Article Title: 2 held for duping 4500 investors of Rs 47cr in fake ponzi scheme in Navi Mumbai
Article Text:
Navi Mumbai police have successfully dismantled a deceptive ponzi scheme run by Ossam Swadesh LLP, which cheated nearly 4500 investors (Representative image)

NAVI MUMBAI: The Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) of Navi Mumbai police have busted a fake ponzi scheme being operated by Ossam Swadesh LLP company having office in Akshar Business Park in Turbhe, wherein as many as 4500 investors have been duped of around Rs 47 crore.The FIU registered an FIR of cheating and criminal breach of trust against seven acccused at APMC police station on Tuesday and have arrested two accused including the company's Chief Managing Director Sunil Palve and a marketing agent Navnath Repale.The other acccused named in the FIR are company's marketing directors Jayendra Gambhe along with Ashish Barve, Amol Jadhav, Dinesh Kolambe and other agents involved in assuring investors of high returns.ACP (crime) Prerana Katte Informed that, the FIU had received an anonymous complaint of the fake investment scheme by Ossam Swadesh LLP company alleging thousands of investors have been duped by offering high returns since Jan 2024.Accordingly, on July 15, a woman cop posing as customer approached the company, when she was told by the marketing agent Repale that so far 21,000 people have invested in the company's scheme offering high returns.He told that if she invested an amount of Rs 1 lakh, she will be given a diamond as gift and Rs 5,000 returns per month for one year and thereafter, Rs 6,000 per month for next 12 months.Apart from that, if she brings 2 new investors within 30 days, she will be given a commission of Rs 6,000/- per month for the next 10 months.Also, after 24 months, if she returns the gifted diamond, the company will refund her Rs 90,000.ACP Katte said, "To lay a trap, the decoy customer invested Rs 1 lakh. As the company did not give her diamond gift, as assured. Subsequently, a raid was conducted on Tuesday and upon checking the documents and computer entries, it was revealed that as many as 4,500 investors were duped of around Rs 47 crore in the fake investment scheme. The case is being further probed by the Economic Offences Wing."
Article Summary: Navi Mumbai police busted a fake ponzi scheme being operated by Ossam Swadesh LLP. As many as 4500 investors have been duped of around Rs 47 crore. FIU registered an FIR of cheating and criminal breach of trust against seven acccused.

Article Title: Technical snag on Navi Mumbai Metro Line-1 disrupts services for 20 mins
Article Text:
A technical snag disrupted the passenger services for nearly 20 minutes on Monday (File Photo)

NAVI MUMBAI: For the first time since the Navi Mumbai Metro Line-1 services between CBD-Belapur and Pendhar, Taloja node was started by Cidco on Nov 17, 2023, a technical snag disrupted the passenger services for nearly 20 minutes on Monday during morning rush hour around 8.30 am.Hundreds of commuters, particularly office-goers, travelling in the metro train from Pendhar towards CBD-Belapur, were stranded at RBI colony metro station near Belpada.An office-goer Narendra Batham (30), a civil engineer residing in Kharghar said, "I boarded the metro train at Central Park station at Kharghar to reach CBD-Belapur. Around 8.30 am, the train halted at RBI Colony station, when there was public announcement by the motorman saying 'Attention passengers. This train has developed a technical snag and cannot proceed further. Please de-board the train carefully and move towards the platform. Follow the instructions of the station staff and await the next train.We apologize for the delay.' The motorman kept the train doors open and all commuters alighted and waited on the platform."Batham added, "After the technical snag was rectified around 8.50 am, the commuters continued their journey by the same train.Since, reaching the CBD-Belapur station by walking would have taken longer time and even auto rickshaws are not easily available near the metro station. However, after the train departed and covered some distance, again a technical snag developed for just 30 seconds and then finally reached the next last station at CBD-Belapur. "Santosh Ombhase, Cidco's superintending engineer for metro project said, "I have sought a detailed report of the technical snag of metro train from the concerned authorities of The Maharashtra Metro Rail Corporation Limited (Maha Metro) who runs and operates the Navi Mumbai Metro Line 1. Also, even if the train halts on via-duct (bridge) far away from the metro station, there is an evacuation plan as there are walkways on both sides for the commuters to walk till the nearest station. However, for safety of commuters, the train doors get locked when the train halts on the bridge due to technical snag and only the motorman can unlock the doors. Also, inside the trains, there is arrangement of wheel chairs for senior citizens and the metro staff helps them in moving safely to the platform."
Article Summary: For the first time since the Navi Mumbai Metro Line-1 services between CBD- Belapur and Pendhar, Taloja node was started by Cidco on Nov 17, 2023, a technical snag disrupted the passenger services for nearly 20 minutes on Monday. Hundreds of commuters, particularly office-goers, were stranded at RBI colony metro station near Belpada.

Article Title: 3 booked for negligence causing death of machine operator at steel factory in Khalapur
Article Text:
3 booked for negligence causing death of machine operator

NAVI MUMBAI: Khalapur police have registered an FIR against three persons including two shift in-charge and a unit head at Hindustan Inox Ltd, a stainless steel manufacturing company located at Kumbhivali, Khalapur taluka, for their alleged negligence causing death of a 50-year-old operator working on an OTO (Overhead Take-Off) machine at the wire unit.The accused have been identified as Shravan Singh (31), Ajay Singh (21), both night shift in-charge and Mukesh Singh (42), who is the wire unit head. They were booked under BNS section for causing death by negligence not amounting to culpable homicide and joint criminal liability.Sachin Pawar, senior inspector of Khalapur said, "The deceased was identified as Suresh Rajendra Choudhary, who hails from Bihar. On July 6 around 2.30 am, while Choudhary was working in night shift met with an accident after his finger got stuck in the running steel wire-drawing machine and got pulled into the machine causing him grievous head injuries. He succumbed to his injuries during treatment at hospital on July 6 around 6 am. Prima facie, an accidental death report was filed and further probe was initiated. It was concluded that the three accused were responsible for the death of Choudhary, as safety equipment was provided while engaging him at the OTO machine. Hence, on Wed, an FIR was registered against the three accused."
Article Summary: Suresh Rajendra Choudhary, 50, was working on an OTO (Overhead Take-Off) machine. His finger got stuck in the running steel wire-drawing machine and got pulled into the machine. He succumbed to his injuries during treatment at hospital on July 6 around 6 am.

Article Title: Cyber fraudsters dupe Navi Mumbai firms of Rs 11 crore by cloning WhatsApp profiles of top executives
Article Text:
Cyber fraudsters allegedly duped a jewellery manufacturing company and a real estate firm in Navi Mumbai

NAVI MUMBAI: In two separate incidents of cyber scammers hijacking Whatsapp numbers and spoofing display profile to impersonate trusted contact, sent messages for transferring money and duped a jewellery manufacturing company and a renowned construction firm in Navi Mumbai, collectively Rs 2 crore.Vishal Patil, senior inspector of Cyber police station informed that, an FIR was registered at Cyber police station on Thursday after a complaint was lodged by Bharat Kumar Chobdar (45), the chief financial officer of Star Mangalsutra LLP, a jewellery manufacturing company in Mahape MIDC.The complainant stated that on July 14 and 15, the scammer hijacked the WhatsApp number of company owner Mangesh Chauhan and using his display profile on another WhatsApp number sent messages to Company's mobile number mentioning to transfer money to different bank accounts provided in the message.Accordingly, the company's accounts department transferred a total of ₹10.70 crore to those accounts, unaware that the messages were sent by a scammer.Accordingly, the unidentified accused and the bank account holders where the money was transferred were booked under relevant BNS sections for cheating by impersonation and cheating by dishonestly inducing the delivery of property and joint criminal liability along with IT Act.Yet another similar cyber fraudster case was registered at Vashi police station on Thursday after a complaint was lodged by Hemant Katkar (48), the liasioning officer of Haware Properties stating that a scammer hijacked the WhatsApp number of the firm's Joint MD-cum-Chief Financial Officer Amar Suresh Haware and using his display profile on another WhatsApp number, sent a message to the company's accounts department staff for transferring Rs 30 lakh to a bank account.The incident occurred between 12.33 pm on July 14 and 1.20 pm on July 15.Accordingly, the accused cyber scammer was booked under relevant BNS sections for cheating and dishonestly inducing the delivery of property and cheating by impersonation along with IT Act.Coincidentally, both the incidents occured on the same days on July 14 and 15. Hence, the police suspect that the same cyber fraudster might be behind the scam.
Article Summary: In two separate incidents of cyber scammers hijacking Whatsapp numbers and spoofing display profile to impersonate trusted contact, sent messages for transferring money. Cyber fraudsters duped a jewellery manufacturing company and a real estate firm in Navi Mumbai, collectively Rs 2 crore.

Article Title: Navi Mumbai cyber fraud: 2 booked for opening mule bank accounts to route scam money
Article Text:
Between December 12 last year and January 1, Harsh allegedly received Rs 61.74 lakh from victims of cyber fraud before transferring the amount to bank accounts operated by cyber criminals. (AI-generated image)

NAVI MUMBAI: Two separate FIRs have been registered at the CBD-Belapur and Vashi police stations against two men for allegedly opening 'mule' bank accounts to receive proceeds of cyber fraud and transfer the money to cyber criminals.Technical analysis by the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal (NCCRP) team and an examination of the money trail by cyber police revealed that one of the accused, Harsh Ankush Nimbalkar, a resident of Sector 15, Sanpada, had allegedly opened a bank account at the Vashi branch of a nationalised bank with the help of his associates. Between December 12 last year and January 1, he allegedly received Rs 61.74 lakh from victims of cyber fraud before transferring the amount to bank accounts operated by cyber criminals.According to information obtained from the NCCRP, Nimbalkar's bank account was linked to 31 cyber fraud complaints registered in different states across the country. The Vashi police booked him under relevant provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) for cheating, dishonestly inducing the delivery of property, and dishonestly receiving or retaining stolen property knowing or having reason to believe it was stolen.In a separate case, another accused, Rahul Ranjit Singh, a resident of Kopra village in Sector 10, Kharghar, allegedly opened a bank account at the Belapur branch of a private sector commercial bank. Police said that between December 17 and 18 last year, Rs 2.57 lakh from victims of cyber fraud was credited to his account, after which he allegedly transferred the money to cyber criminals after deducting his commission.The CBD-Belapur police booked him for allegedly assisting cyber criminals in the embezzlement and distribution of the crime proceeds and concealing criminal financial transactions.Arun Pawar, senior inspector of CBD-Belapur police station, said, "Prima facie, it appears that the accused Rahul Ranjit Singh is an active member of the organized cyber criminal gang. Hence, a criminal case was registered against him and other unknown persons who were handling the said bank account."
Article Summary: Harsh Ankush Nimbalkar allegedly opened a bank account at the Vashi branch of a nationalised bank with the help of his associates. He allegedly received Rs 61.74 lakh from victims of cyber fraud before transferring the amount to bank accounts operated by cyber criminals. In a separate case, Rahul Ranjit Singh, a resident of Kopra village in Sector 10, Kharghar, allegedly opened an account.
